title: fraxinus excelsior f. erosa
intro: Fraxinus excelsior f. erosa, also known as the ash wych elm or field ash, is a variant of the common ash tree, characterized by its unique combination of Fraxinus excelsior and Ulmus minor traits. The medicinal properties of Fraxinus excelsior f. erosa lie in its bark, leaves, and twigs, which contain compounds like salicin, a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory agent. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe sore muscles, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Fraxinus excelsior f. erosa can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used in traditional folk medicine to promote relaxation and relieve stress.
